name: Corner Brook
summary: Corner Brook is a small city tucked into a sparkling fjord on the west coast of Newfoundland, Canada. It's the gateway to Gros Morne National Park, where huge cliffs, fjords and orange rocks make the land look like another planet.
funFacts: Captain James Cook mapped the bays around Corner Brook in the 1760s, and his sea charts were so good they were still being used 100 years later.
At the Tablelands in Gros Morne, the rusty orange rocks are pieces of Earth's mantle pushed up from 30 kilometres underground — almost no other place lets you walk on them.
Western Brook Pond is a freshwater fjord with cliffs about 1.2 billion years old, carved out by giant Ice Age glaciers.
Newfoundland has more moose per square kilometre than anywhere else in the world, even though only four were brought to the island in 1904.
Marble Mountain just outside Corner Brook gets some of the deepest snow in eastern Canada, with about 5 metres falling each winter.
thingsToDo: Climb Crow Hill to the Captain James Cook Monument and look out over the whole sparkling Bay of Islands.
Drive an hour north into Gros Morne National Park and walk on the orange Tablelands rocks from deep inside the Earth.
Take a boat tour through Western Brook Pond fjord, gliding between billion-year-old cliffs and tall waterfalls.
Ski, snowboard or zipline at Marble Mountain, Atlantic Canada's biggest ski hill, just ten minutes from town.
Visit Lobster Cove Head Lighthouse and look for whales, seabirds and icebergs out in the Atlantic Ocean.
Picnic at Margaret Bowater Park downtown, a leafy spot with gardens, a pool and gentle walking paths.

wildlife.name: Moose
wildlife.description: Huge antlered deer that munches leaves in the forest — Newfoundland has more moose than anywhere else.

wildlife.name: Newfoundland Caribou
wildlife.description: Shaggy reindeer cousin with curved antlers that wanders the open hills of Gros Morne.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean animal that visits the bay each summer and sometimes leaps right out of the water.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Chubby black-and-white seabird with a bright orange beak — Newfoundland's official provincial bird.

localWord.meaning: Newfoundland slang meaning 'excellent' or 'doing great' — say it when someone asks how you are.

images.alt: A bird's-eye view of Corner Brook tucked beside the sparkling waters of the Bay of Islands fjord.
A glowing orange sunset over the harbour seen from the Captain James Cook lookout in Corner Brook.
The bare orange Tablelands mountains, made of rock from deep inside the Earth, look like another planet.
Towering grey cliffs rise straight out of the still blue water of Western Brook Pond's hidden fjord.
Snowy ski runs and chairlifts on Marble Mountain just outside Corner Brook.
The red-and-white Lobster Cove Head Lighthouse standing on rocks above the sea in Gros Morne National Park.
A big bull moose with huge antlers stands among the grass in Gros Morne National Park.
A shaggy woodland caribou with curved antlers standing in a mountain meadow.
A humpback whale leaping out of the ocean.
An Atlantic puffin with a bright orange beak standing on a grassy cliff.
